name: Russia
headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — death toll from the May 14-15 mass strike rose from 1 to 24 civilians killed per Euronews May 15 confirmation. 9-story residential building in Darnytsia (Kyiv) collapsed, destroying all 18 apartments. Multi-city strikes: Kyiv, Odesa, Kherson, Kharkiv. 675 attack drones + 56 missiles deployed in single overnight attack; 652 drones and 41 missiles intercepted; conducted as the largest single-day combined drone/missile assault of the post-ceasefire period. Macron (May 15): Russia's attack 'laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce.' This is a 24x death-toll revision from the May 14 first-cycle report, confirming the assessment that the May 14-15 attack package extends the post-ceasefire 'large-scale escalation' phase documented yesterday. Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; documentation updated with the revised casualty figure and Macron major-state characterization.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. A1 detection: the May 14-15 attack targeted residential infrastructure with no documented military-objective justification; awareness of civilian-harm impact was not absent (the targets were known), it was disregarded.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 24 civilian deaths confirmed; 9-story residential building collapsed; no state-level recognition of civilian harm; no reparative response.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC1 responsiveness: no response to civilian harm caused by Russian action; AC2 proportionality: 675-drone/56-missile single-night attack against multiple cities targeting residential infrastructure is structurally disproportionate.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Civilian populations targeted indiscriminately across multiple cities; no equity-dimension protective infrastructure documented.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. International-law boundary infrastructure (UN Charter Article 2(4), IHL on civilian protection) violated; ICC jurisdiction engaged (carry-over).

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No accountability mechanism for the May 14-15 attack; no investigation, no transparency, no correction-willingness. Macron major-state characterization ('hypocrisy') is external accountability invocation but does not produce ACC-dimension uplift for the actor under scrutiny.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. The May 14-15 attack establishes a 4th-phase post-ceasefire offensive surge ('large-scale escalation' beyond sustain), now corroborated by 24-death revision. Systems-level pattern of bad-faith ceasefire-format conduct continues.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. The May 14-15 attack occurred during active ceasefire negotiations — Macron 'laid bare the hypocrisy' framing is a major-state INT-dimension characterization. Negotiation-concurrent attack is the canonical integrity floor evidence.

evidence.summary: Euronews May 15: death toll from May 14 Russian mass attack rose to 24 civilians killed (up from initial report of 1). 675 attack drones + 56 missiles in single overnight attack; 9-story residential building collapsed in Darnytsia (Kyiv); multi-city strikes Kyiv/Odesa/Kherson/Kharkiv.
EMPR Media Russia-Ukraine war updates as of May 15 — corroborate the multi-city strike package and the residential-building collapse.
EMPR Media Russia-Ukraine war updates as of May 14 — establish the timeline of the strike-night-to-large-scale-escalation transition.
Macron May 15: Russia's attack 'laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce.' Major-state INT-dimension characterization of the negotiation-concurrent attack pattern.

keyEvidence: May 14-15: 24 civilians killed (revised from 1) per Euronews May 15; 675 drones + 56 missiles single overnight attack.
9-story residential building in Darnytsia (Kyiv) collapsed — all 18 apartments destroyed.
Multi-city strikes: Kyiv, Odesa, Kherson, Kharkiv. 652 drones and 41 missiles intercepted.
Macron (May 15): attack 'laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce' — major-state INT characterization.
Largest single-day combined drone/missile attack of the post-ceasefire period — extends to 4th phase ('large-scale escalation') beyond sustain.
Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; new conduct documentation added.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The May 14-15 attack with 24-death revised toll, residential-building collapse, and multi-city strike pattern is the largest single-day civilian-casualty event of the post-ceasefire period. The negotiation-concurrent timing — attacks intensified during active ceasefire talks — is the canonical INT floor signal, now corroborated by Macron's major-state 'hypocrisy' characterization. All eight dimensions remain at anchor 1; no score change; new conduct documentation added for the 4th-phase post-ceasefire offensive-surge category ('large-scale escalation' beyond sustain). Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: International accountability mechanism activation (ICC, UN Human Rights Council)
Ceasefire negotiation status — whether negotiations continue concurrently with attacks
Documented civilian-infrastructure targeting (residential buildings as primary target)
European major-state response pattern (Macron May 15 framing as bellwether)
carryForward: Post-ceasefire offensive-surge documentation (4 phases now established)
Negotiation-concurrent attack pattern as INT floor evidence
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: the Macron May 15 'hypocrisy' framing introduces a new sub-anchor for INT-dimension floor evidence — 'major-state-bellwether-characterization-of-bad-faith-conduct.' When a comparable major state (G7/G20 member) on-record characterizes negotiation-concurrent attack conduct as hypocrisy, this is a particularly strong INT floor signal because it represents external INT-credibility deficit from a peer rather than from an asymmetrically-affected smaller state.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not applicable — floor entity; no boundary risk; no math drift.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. 24-death revised toll compounds floor-exit evidentiary burden but does not affect composite arithmetic.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
